* Music video director * graphic designer * singer-songwriter

(French pronunciation: [[jɔan ləmwan]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French"); born 16 March 1983), known professionally as **Woodkid**, is a French [music video director](/wiki/Music_video_director "Music video director"), [graphic designer](/wiki/Graphic_designer "Graphic designer") and singer-songwriter.[[5]](#cite_note-Nationality-5)[[6]](#cite_note-OfficialWebsiteAboutPage-6) His most notable works include his music video direction for [Katy Perry](/wiki/Katy_Perry "Katy Perry")'s "[Teenage Dream](/wiki/Teenage_Dream_(Katy_Perry_song) "Teenage Dream (Katy Perry song)")", [Taylor Swift](/wiki/Taylor_Swift "Taylor Swift")'s "[Back to December](/wiki/Back_to_December "Back to December")", [Drake](/wiki/Drake_(musician) "Drake (musician)")'s "[Take Care](/wiki/Take_Care_(song) "Take Care (song)")", [Lana Del Rey](/wiki/Lana_Del_Rey "Lana Del Rey")'s "[Born to Die](/wiki/Born_to_Die_(song) "Born to Die (song)")" and [Harry Styles](/wiki/Harry_Styles "Harry Styles")'s "[Sign of the Times](/wiki/Sign_of_the_Times_(Harry_Styles_song) "Sign of the Times (Harry Styles song)")".

Musical artist

**Yoann Lemoine** (French pronunciation: [[jɔan ləmwan]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French"); born 16 March 1983), known professionally as **Woodkid**, is a French [music video director](/wiki/Music_video_director "Music video director"), [graphic designer](/wiki/Graphic_designer "Graphic designer") and singer-songwriter.[[5]](#cite_note-Nationality-5)[[6]](#cite_note-OfficialWebsiteAboutPage-6) His most notable works include his music video direction for [Katy Perry](/wiki/Katy_Perry "Katy Perry")'s "[Teenage Dream](/wiki/Teenage_Dream_(Katy_Perry_song) "Teenage Dream (Katy Perry song)")", [Taylor Swift](/wiki/Taylor_Swift "Taylor Swift")'s "[Back to December](/wiki/Back_to_December "Back to December")", [Drake](/wiki/Drake_(musician) "Drake (musician)")'s "[Take Care](/wiki/Take_Care_(song) "Take Care (song)")", [Lana Del Rey](/wiki/Lana_Del_Rey "Lana Del Rey")'s "[Born to Die](/wiki/Born_to_Die_(song) "Born to Die (song)")" and [Harry Styles](/wiki/Harry_Styles "Harry Styles")'s "[Sign of the Times](/wiki/Sign_of_the_Times_(Harry_Styles_song) "Sign of the Times (Harry Styles song)")".

Lemoine is also a [chamber pop](/wiki/Chamber_pop "Chamber pop") musician.[[7]](#cite_note-7) On 28 March 2011, he released his first [EP](/wiki/EP "EP") *Iron EP*.[[8]](#cite_note-Visco-8) On 18 March 2013, he released his first album titled *[The Golden Age](/wiki/The_Golden_Age_(Woodkid_album) "The Golden Age (Woodkid album)")*, which is an autobiographical record.[[9]](#cite_note-9) He released his second album *S16* on 16 October 2020, more than seven years later, following some single releases. His third album, a collaboration with [Hideo Kojima](/wiki/Hideo_Kojima "Hideo Kojima") for the 2025 video game *[Death Stranding 2: On the Beach](/wiki/Death_Stranding_2:_On_the_Beach "Death Stranding 2: On the Beach")*, was released on 13 June 2025.[[10]](#cite_note-:1-10) He has also composed for the series *[Arcane](/wiki/Arcane_(TV_series) "Arcane (TV series)")*.[[11]](#cite_note-:2-11)

## Life and career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Woodkid&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Life and career")]

### Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Woodkid&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Early life")]

Yoann Lemoine was born in [Tassin-la-Demi-Lune](/wiki/Tassin-la-Demi-Lune "Tassin-la-Demi-Lune"), near [Lyon](/wiki/Lyon "Lyon"),[[12]](#cite_note-12) to a mother of [Polish-Jewish](/wiki/Polish-Jewish "Polish-Jewish") origin, and a father working in advertising.[[13]](#cite_note-:0-13) He studied illustration and animation at the Émile Cohl school, where he completed his diploma with honors. He then left for the UK to follow a silk screen printing process course at [Swindon College](/wiki/Swindon_College "Swindon College"). In 2004, Yoann moved to [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris"). After a brief experience at [H5](/wiki/H5_(French_company) "H5 (French company)"), he joined [Luc Besson](/wiki/Luc_Besson "Luc Besson")'s crew and worked on the project *[Arthur and the Invisibles](/wiki/Arthur_and_the_Invisibles "Arthur and the Invisibles")* for a year. In 2006, Yoann directed a series of roughs for [Sofia Coppola](/wiki/Sofia_Coppola "Sofia Coppola")'s *[Marie Antoinette](/wiki/Marie_Antoinette_(2006_film) "Marie Antoinette (2006 film)")*.[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/ff/Woodkid_zurich_2013.jpg/250px-Woodkid_zurich_2013.jpg)](/wiki/File:Woodkid_zurich_2013.jpg)

Woodkid performing at the Kaufleuten in Zurich, 2013

### 2011–2019: Beginnings, *The Golden Age*, and other ventures

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Woodkid&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: 2011–2019: Beginnings, The Golden Age, and other ventures")]

His first musical project is about the transition from childhood to adulthood and his countryside background. The songs can be described as 'organic' and wooden. The project is about a kid, who starts organic and later turns himself into marble.[[8]](#cite_note-Visco-8) One explanation as to why Lemoine became a musician is that guitarist [Richie Havens](/wiki/Richie_Havens "Richie Havens") gave him a ukulele during a video shoot he was directing.[[16]](#cite_note-airfrance-16) Woodkid released the *Iron EP* on 28 March 2011.[[8]](#cite_note-Visco-8) For the music video for the single "Iron" (composed by Yoann Lemoine), he collaborated with English model [Agyness Deyn](/wiki/Agyness_Deyn "Agyness Deyn").[[17]](#cite_note-AgynessDeyn-17) The song was featured in trailers for [Ubisoft](/wiki/Ubisoft "Ubisoft")'s *[Assassin's Creed: Revelations](/wiki/Assassin%27s_Creed:_Revelations "Assassin's Creed: Revelations")*[[18]](#cite_note-18) and the film *[Hitchcock](/wiki/Hitchcock_(film) "Hitchcock (film)")* as well as the TV series *[Teen Wolf](/wiki/Teen_Wolf_(2011_TV_series) "Teen Wolf (2011 TV series)")*.

On 6 October 2011, during a live performance in [Brussels](/wiki/Brussels "Brussels"), he performed a cover of the [Katy Perry](/wiki/Katy_Perry "Katy Perry") song "[Teenage Dream](/wiki/Teenage_Dream_(Katy_Perry_song) "Teenage Dream (Katy Perry song)")"[[19]](#cite_note-19) (whose official video he directed). On 15 October 2011, he sang on stage with [Lana Del Rey](/wiki/Lana_Del_Rey "Lana Del Rey") in New York City.[[20]](#cite_note-20)[[21]](#cite_note-LanaDelReyWorldTour-21) He co-created the video for her song "Born to Die".[[22]](#cite_note-22) He performed at the [London Jazz Festival](/wiki/London_Jazz_Festival "London Jazz Festival") on 15 November 2012.[[23]](#cite_note-southbank-23)

The French couture brand [Dior Homme](/wiki/Dior_Homme "Dior Homme")'s fall-winter 2013 collection, "A Soldier on My Own", was inspired by Woodkid's "Iron", and is named after a phrase in the lyrics. The song was used as the soundtrack of the show.[[24]](#cite_note-WoodkidForDior-24)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/37/Woodkid_and_Philip_Glass.jpg/250px-Woodkid_and_Philip_Glass.jpg)](/wiki/File:Woodkid_and_Philip_Glass.jpg)

Woodkid and Philip Glass after their conference about New York Minimalism at the Opera de Saint Etienne in 2014

On 15 December 2012, Lemoine announced he was beginning recording of his debut album, to be named *The Golden Age*.[[25]](#cite_note-25) It was released on 18 March 2013 through independent label, Green United Music. Lemoine released the official album cover through a video he created himself on 14 December 2012[[26]](#cite_note-26) and released the album's corresponding track listing one week later on 21 December on his official Facebook page.[[27]](#cite_note-27)

"[Run Boy Run](/wiki/Run_Boy_Run_(song) "Run Boy Run (song)")" was directed by Lemoine himself[[28]](#cite_note-28) and was used in several [O2](/wiki/O2_(United_Kingdom) "O2 (United Kingdom)") and [Science Channel](/wiki/Science_Channel "Science Channel") adverts (those voiced by [Sean Bean](/wiki/Sean_Bean "Sean Bean")).[[29]](#cite_note-Run_Boy_Run-29)[[30]](#cite_note-Science_Channel_Commercial-30) It was also used in the teaser trailers for the BBC's series *[The Musketeers](/wiki/The_Musketeers "The Musketeers")*, in January 2014.[[31]](#cite_note-31) The music video for "Run Boy Run" was nominated for [Best Short Form Music Video](/wiki/Best_Short_Form_Music_Video "Best Short Form Music Video") at the [2013 Grammy Awards](/wiki/2013_Grammy_Awards "2013 Grammy Awards"). The song was also used in the trailer for the game *[Dying Light](/wiki/Dying_Light "Dying Light")*. "Run Boy Run" and "[I Love You](/wiki/I_Love_You_(Woodkid_song) "I Love You (Woodkid song)")" were featured on the soundtrack of the 2014 film *[Divergent](/wiki/Divergent:_Original_Motion_Picture_Soundtrack "Divergent: Original Motion Picture Soundtrack")*. During his Golden Age tour, Woodkid performed at the London [Brixton Academy](/wiki/Brixton_Academy "Brixton Academy") with the [BBC Concert Orchestra](/wiki/BBC_Concert_Orchestra "BBC Concert Orchestra")[[32]](#cite_note-32) in November 2013,[[33]](#cite_note-Woodkid_at_Brixton_Academy-33) and was invited by the [Montreux Jazz Festival](/wiki/Montreux_Jazz_Festival "Montreux Jazz Festival") to perform for the second time with the Sinfonietta de Lausanne at the Stravinsky Auditorium.[[34]](#cite_note-Woodkid_at_Montreux_Jazz_Festival-34) The Golden Age tour performances were marked by a well-coordinated light show and visual projections that included imagery from his music videos.[[35]](#cite_note-35) The song is also used in the Umbrella Academy, in connection to Number 5, and the episode was also called Run Boy Run.

In January 2014, Lemoine / Woodkid was invited to give a performance and talk about the New York minimalist movement with [Philip Glass](/wiki/Philip_Glass "Philip Glass") by the Opéra and Museum of Modern Art of Saint-Étienne.[[36]](#cite_note-Table_Ronde_en_présence_de_Philip_Glass_et_Woodkid-36) In February 2014, Woodkid received the award for best stage performer at the French equivalent of the Grammy Awards, Les [Victoires de la Musique](/wiki/Victoires_de_la_Musique "Victoires de la Musique").[[37]](#cite_note-Woodkid_récompensé_aux_Victoires_de_la_Musique-37) In 2014, Woodkid / Lemoine worked as creative director for [Pharrell Williams](/wiki/Pharrell_Williams "Pharrell Williams")' 24 Hours of [Happy](/wiki/Happy_(Pharrell_Williams_song) "Happy (Pharrell Williams song)") music video concept, seen more than 600 million times on YouTube.[[38]](#cite_note-24_hours_of_Happy_Credits-38)[[39]](#cite_note-39) He also served as creative director of the video campaign for [John Legend](/wiki/John_Legend "John Legend")'s album *[Love in the Future](/wiki/Love_in_the_Future "Love in the Future")*, produced by [Kanye West](/wiki/Kanye_West "Kanye West"), including videos for "Who Do We Think We Are", directed by Paul Gore[[40]](#cite_note-John_Legend_-_Who_do_we_think_we_are_Credits_on_Youtube-40) and "Made to Love", directed by [Daniel Sannwald](/wiki/Daniel_Sannwald "Daniel Sannwald").[[41]](#cite_note-Yoann_Lemoine_for_Made_to_Love_on_MY_BAND_MARKET-41)

In April 2014, Woodkid performed at [Coachella](/wiki/Coachella "Coachella").[[42]](#cite_note-42) On 26 June 2014, Woodkid opened the [Montreal International Jazz Festival](/wiki/Montreal_International_Jazz_Festival "Montreal International Jazz Festival") with a free outdoor concert to an audience of 100,000+.[[43]](#cite_note-43) In July 2014, Ubisoft released its second cinematic trailer for *[Assassin's Creed Unity](/wiki/Assassin%27s_Creed_Unity "Assassin's Creed Unity")*, where "The Golden Age" was played. In August 2014, Woodkid performed at the [FM4 Frequency Festival](/wiki/FM4_Frequency_Festival "FM4 Frequency Festival") in Austria. In December 2014, Woodkid performed at [Wonderfruit](/wiki/Wonderfruit "Wonderfruit") in Thailand. He also directed [Pharrell Williams](/wiki/Pharrell_Williams "Pharrell Williams")'s Coachella show, featuring [Jay Z](/wiki/Jay_Z "Jay Z"), [Gwen Stefani](/wiki/Gwen_Stefani "Gwen Stefani"), [Usher](/wiki/Usher_(entertainer) "Usher (entertainer)"), [Pusha T](/wiki/Pusha_T "Pusha T"), [Busta Rhymes](/wiki/Busta_Rhymes "Busta Rhymes"), and [Puff Daddy](/wiki/Puff_Daddy "Puff Daddy").[[44]](#cite_note-44) [The Cavaliers Drum and Bugle Corps](/wiki/The_Cavaliers_Drum_and_Bugle_Corps "The Cavaliers Drum and Bugle Corps") featured "Run Boy Run" as part of their 2015 repertoire, entitled "Game On".

On 15 July 2016, Woodkid performed at the [Montreux Jazz Festival](/wiki/Montreux_Jazz_Festival "Montreux Jazz Festival") at a special "Woodkid and Friends" evening with a nearly all acoustic set. He was accompanied by the Sinfonietta de Lausanne orchestra and a choir of children from the Montreux Choral Festival. Among his friends who also performed at this event were [Son Lux](/wiki/Son_Lux "Son Lux"), [The Shoes](/wiki/The_Shoes_(French_band) "The Shoes (French band)"), [Ed Droste](/wiki/Ed_Droste "Ed Droste"), [Thomas Bloch](/wiki/Thomas_Bloch "Thomas Bloch"), and actress [Elle Fanning](/wiki/Elle_Fanning "Elle Fanning") singing for the first time live on a stage.[[45]](#cite_note-45) On 16 September 2016, Woodkid promoted [Mykki Blanco](/wiki/Mykki_Blanco "Mykki Blanco")'s debut album on his Facebook page. He is featured on the single "High School Never Ends" and helped produce the album. In March 2017, the second episode of *[13 Reasons Why](/wiki/13_Reasons_Why "13 Reasons Why")* aired, featuring "Run Boy Run". In February 2019, the second episode of *[The Umbrella Academy](/wiki/The_Umbrella_Academy_(TV_series) "The Umbrella Academy (TV series)")* aired, which also featured "Run Boy Run" and used the song as the episode's namesake. In May 2022, the fifth episode of *[The Pentaverate](/wiki/The_Pentaverate "The Pentaverate")* aired, which also featured "Run Boy Run".

### 2019–present: S*16*, *Arcane* and *Death Stranding*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Woodkid&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 2019–present: S16, Arcane and Death Stranding")]

On 7 May 2019, Woodkid released the EP *Woodkid For [Nicolas Ghesquière](/wiki/Nicolas_Ghesqui%C3%A8re "Nicolas Ghesquière") – [Louis Vuitton](/wiki/Louis_Vuitton "Louis Vuitton") Works One*, featuring [Jennifer Connelly](/wiki/Jennifer_Connelly "Jennifer Connelly") and [Moses Sumney](/wiki/Moses_Sumney "Moses Sumney"). On 12 December 2019, Woodkid announced the production of his second album and that it was set to release in 2020. On 24 April 2020, Woodkid released "Goliath", the first single from his upcoming album.[[46]](#cite_note-46) The album, titled "S16", was released on 16 October 2020.[[47]](#cite_note-nastylittleman-47)

In 2021, Woodkid teamed up with [Paris 2024](/wiki/Paris_2024 "Paris 2024") to compose a new song called "Prologue", this song was featured in the [closing ceremony of the Olympic Games Tokyo 2020](/wiki/2020_Summer_Olympics_closing_ceremony "2020 Summer Olympics closing ceremony") and the [2020 Summer Paralympics closing ceremony](/wiki/2020_Summer_Paralympics_closing_ceremony "2020 Summer Paralympics closing ceremony") and was released on digital platforms on 8 August of that year. On 30 November 2021, Woodkid made a post threatening legal action concerning the use of his music in propaganda from a group supporting politician [Éric Zemmour](/wiki/%C3%89ric_Zemmour "Éric Zemmour"). The post quickly gained popularity, gathering over 48,000 likes in just 7 hours.[[48]](#cite_note-48)

On 27 October 2021, Woodkid announced on Twitter[[49]](#cite_note-49) that he had made an original song for the [Riot Games](/wiki/Riot_Games "Riot Games") series *[Arcane](/wiki/Arcane_(TV_Series) "Arcane (TV Series)")*. The song in question, "Guns For Hire", was featured in the sixth episode of the series released in November 2021. Woodkid returned to compose for *Arcane* for its second season on 9 November 2024, writing another original song featured in the third episode, called "To Ashes and Blood".[[11]](#cite_note-:2-11)

In 2022, Woodkid collaborated with French singer-songwriter [Mylène Farmer](/wiki/Myl%C3%A8ne_Farmer "Mylène Farmer") on her twelfth album, "[L'Emprise](/wiki/L%27Emprise "L'Emprise")", producing more than half of the album. He also provided the artwork for the album.

On 9 March 2025, Woodkid's role as the co-composer of [Hideo Kojima](/wiki/Hideo_Kojima "Hideo Kojima")'s 2025 video game *[Death Stranding 2: On the Beach](/wiki/Death_Stranding_2:_On_the_Beach "Death Stranding 2: On the Beach")*, alongside Ludvig Forssell, was revealed, with his original song "To the Wilder" being featured in the game's trailer.[[50]](#cite_note-50) Woodkid reportedly worked on the game's soundtrack for three years, occasionally travelling to [Tokyo](/wiki/Tokyo "Tokyo") to work in-house at [Kojima Productions](/wiki/Kojima_Productions "Kojima Productions")' studio.[[51]](#cite_note-51) He announced his album for *Death Stranding 2* in collaboration with Hideo Kojima on 8 June 2025, and it released on 13 June 2025 with sixteen tracks including eleven original songs, featuring actress [Elle Fanning](/wiki/Elle_Fanning "Elle Fanning") and composer [Bryce Dessner](/wiki/Bryce_Dessner "Bryce Dessner").[[10]](#cite_note-:1-10) Some of the music he composed for the game is [procedural](/wiki/Adaptive_music "Adaptive music"), and dynamically adapts to the player's actions.[[52]](#cite_note-52) Woodkid also makes a cameo as a minor character in the game.[[53]](#cite_note-53) For his soundtrack album for *Death Stranding 2*, Woodkid was jointly nominated alongside Ludvig Forsell for the "Best Score & Music" award at [The Game Awards 2025](/wiki/The_Game_Awards_2025 "The Game Awards 2025").[[54]](#cite_note-54)

## Directing and awards

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Woodkid&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Directing and awards")]

In June 2010, he received 5 Lions for his AIDS Awareness campaign *Graffiti* at the [Cannes Lions International Advertising Festival](/wiki/Cannes_Lions_International_Advertising_Festival "Cannes Lions International Advertising Festival").[[55]](#cite_note-OfficialWebsiteAwardsPage-55) His films are produced by Iconoclast.[[56]](#cite_note-IconoclastPage-56)

In 2012, Lemoine received the award for Best Director of the Year at the MVPA Awards in Los Angeles[[57]](#cite_note-MVPAAwardWinnersOnVideoStatic-57) and was nominated for 6 MTV Video Music awards for his videos for Lana Del Rey, Drake, and Rihanna.[[58]](#cite_note-MTVVMAS2012Nominees-58)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Woodkid&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Personal life")]

In a 2014 interview with *[Interview Magazine](/wiki/Interview_Magazine "Interview Magazine")*, Lemoine explained that growing up as a gay kid made him want to leave the countryside for the city, where he could find people like him. He also said he had long been fascinated by America "in a positive and negative way," which motivated his move to the United States.[[59]](#cite_note-59)

His 2012 single "[Run Boy Run](/wiki/Run_Boy_Run_(song) "Run Boy Run (song)")" has been described as "a rousing anthem dealing with transformation and the pursuit of personal freedom, about a misfit who must break free from rigid societal norms." Lemoine has said that the song was inspired by his experiences as a gay man. In 2024, during the [United States presidential election campaign](/wiki/2024_United_States_presidential_election "2024 United States presidential election"), he criticized [Donald Trump](/wiki/Donald_Trump "Donald Trump") for using "Run Boy Run" in campaign rallies and videos without authorization. Lemoine described the track as "a LGBT+ anthem wrote by me, a proud LGBT+ musician," and urged his label not to be complicit.[[60]](#cite_note-60)
